ddls:
CREATE TABLE "game_servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each game server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the game server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the game server */,
  "server_status" TEXT /* Current status of the server (e.g., online, maintenance, offline) */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of server (e.g., public, private, beta) */,
  "max_players" INTEGER /* Maximum number of players the server can support */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the server was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the server was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "game_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each gameplay statistic */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* ID of the game server the statistic belongs to */,
  "table_number" INTEGER /* Table number of the statistic (e.g., 11, 12, etc.) */,
  "players" INTEGER /* Number of players in the game */,
  "pages" INTEGER /* Number of pages (or levels) completed */,
  "total_kc" INTEGER /* Total kills (KC) in the game */,
  "average_kc_per_player" REAL /* Average kills per player */,
  "game_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the game session */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the game session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the game session */,
  "total_deaths" INTEGER /* Total number of player deaths */,
  "average_deaths_per_player" REAL /* Average number of deaths per player */,
  "total_score" INTEGER /* Total score achieved in the game session */,
  "average_score_per_player" REAL /* Average score per player */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the statistic was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the statistic was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_game_statistics_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES game_servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, game developer, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed version of the user's password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* ID of the gameplay statistic being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the statistic */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the statistic was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client making the request */,
  "is_successful" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the access attempt was successful */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the access log was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_stat_id FOREIGN KEY ("stat_id") REFERENCES game_statistics ("stat_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_query" TEXT /* Query used to generate the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the report */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the report is accessible to all users or only to specific roles */,
  "last_run" TEXT /* Last time the report was run */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the report was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the report was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
